title: Devasi Opening — Maṅgalācaraṇa and Guru-sthāpanā
summary: This opening step sets the stage for the daily (Daivasika) pratikramaṇa: it explains why a śrāvaka should perform pratikramaṇa and sāmāyika before the Deva and Guru, prepares the place and clean inspected cloth, and establishes the guru's presence (guru-sthāpanā) so the rite can begin. It culminates in reciting one Navkar and the request to recite Pañchindiya.

segments.plainMeaning: Every morning and evening a śrāvaka should bow to the guru and do pratikramaṇa to confess faults, and should establish the Deva and Guru before performing the rite.
segments.idiomaticTranslation: As the scriptures teach, out of devotion to the worthy one should bow to the Guru Mahārāja; and to confess their faults and to purify all transgressions, śrāvakas should without fail perform pratikramaṇa every evening and morning, and perform sāmāyika daily at a suitable time. All such ritual observances should be done — and done in the very presence of the Deva and Guru. But if they are not physically present, then the whole observance should be done before a Jina-image, the true and the symbolic establishment (sadbhāva- and asadbhāva-sthāpanā) of the guru; and if even that is lacking, a book of knowledge should be set in the ācārya's place and the whole rite performed there, for there is no substance higher than knowledge. In this fifth, difficult age (duḥṣama-kāla), for the upliftment of worthy souls drowned in the darkness of ignorance, Dādā Shrī Āryarakṣitasūrīśvara — forty-seventh in succession from Shrī Sudharmāsvāmī, like a sun in the Jina-order and ornament of the Vidhipakṣa-gaccha — set forth, in accordance with the Āgamas, the pure procedure (śuddha vidhi) for śrāvakas. Accordingly, for the purity of samyaktva, one should now establish the guru with the vidhi described hereafter and then perform sāmāyika and pratikramaṇa.
segments.explanation: A framing maṅgalācaraṇa that gives the rationale and the order of substitution for where the rite may be performed (Deva-Guru → Jina-image / sthāpanā → book of knowledge), and credits the gaccha's codifier Āryarakṣitasūri.

ritualTerms.gloss: the daily rite of turning back from faults through confession and atonement
the 48-minute vow of equanimity
a 'true' (representational) establishment of the guru's presence
a 'symbolic' (non-representational) establishment of the guru's presence
right faith / correct insight
the pure ritual procedure codified for śrāvakas

segments.plainMeaning: If the guru's own inspected sthāpanācārya is already present, do not set up a separate guru-establishment.
segments.idiomaticTranslation: If the Guru Mahārāja's properly inspected (paḍilehaṇa-done) sthāpanācārya is present, then one should not perform the guru-sthāpanā.
segments.explanation: A conditional exception: an existing, inspected sthāpanācārya already serves as the guru's seat, so the establishment is omitted.

ritualTerms.gloss: the ritual object that represents/seats the guru's presence
the prescribed visual inspection of cloth or objects for living beings
the act of establishing the guru's presence for the rite

segments.plainMeaning: Come to the upāśraya, sweep the place, put on clean inspected cloth, set up the seat for the guru's establishment, place the sthāpanā object on it, and recite one Navkār.
segments.idiomaticTranslation: For this, the śrāvaka, going carefully (with jayaṇā) from home to the upāśraya or dharmaśālā, should first sweep the room's gathered dust with the daṇḍāsana; then, disposing of that sweepings carefully, put on clean cloth first inspected by the eye (dṛṣṭi-paḍilehaṇa). Making one sāḍī (of a single width) into the uttarāsaṅga, spreading the kaṭāsaṇu, and carefully wiping a low seat (pāṭ or bājoṭh) for the raised seat of the guru-establishment, set it up; upon it keep a ṭhavaṇī / sāpaḍo and on that establish the consecrated sthāpanācāryajī — or, in its absence, a book of knowledge or a navkārvāḷī (rosary). That is, standing on the kaṭāsaṇu, placing the end of the uttarāsaṅga across the mouth with the left hand and keeping the right hand level facing the sthāpanājī, recite one 'Navkār' aloud.
segments.explanation: The hands-on setup: sweep, dispose of sweepings, don inspected cloth, arrange uttarāsaṅga, kaṭāsaṇu and the raised seat, establish the sthāpanā object, then assume the posture and recite a single Navkār. This segment leads into the Navkār.

ritualTerms.gloss: carefulness to avoid harming living beings
the monks' lodging / hall used for the rite
the soft-tipped staff-broom used for careful sweeping
inspection of cloth by sight before use
the upper cloth draped over the body for the rite
the seating-mat one stands or sits on
the object enthroning the guru's presence

segments.plainMeaning: The disciple asks the guru's leave: 'With your permission, sir — shall I recite the guru-establishment text?'
segments.idiomaticTranslation: By your gracious leave, command me, O Bhagavan! — 'May I recite the guru-establishment text (Pañchindiya)?'
segments.explanation: The disciple's ādeśa (formal request) seeking the guru's sanction before reciting the guru-sthāpanā text; it introduces Pañchindiya.

ritualTerms.gloss: a formal call/request for permission in the call-and-response of the rite
the text recited to establish the guru's presence (Pañchindiya)

segments.explanation: The guru's response granting permission, completing the ādeśa exchange so the disciple may proceed.

ritualTerms.gloss: the call-and-response permission exchange between disciple and guru

segments.idiomaticTranslation: After that, recite the text of Pañchindiya.
segments.explanation: The cue that carries the disciple into the Pañchindiya sūtra, which establishes the guru by enumerating the guru's thirty-six attributes.

ritualTerms.gloss: the sūtra of the guru's 36 attributes, used for guru-sthāpanā

notes: Procedural vidhi rendered per policy (idiomatic + short explanation; no word-by-word/etymology/part-of-speech). Recitation for adesh segments 04–05 transliterates the spoken Prakrit/Gujarati phrase into IAST; for directions 03 and 06 the chantable cue is the named sūtra (Navkār / Pañchindiya), which is itself reproduced under its own sutraId. Segment 01's '47th paṭṭa from Sudharmāsvāmī' and the Āryarakṣitasūri / Vidhipakṣa-gaccha attribution match the tradition KB. No steps, counts, or declarations were added beyond the native text. The footnote marker (¹) on 'Jina-image' in segment 01 was not expanded, as the footnote text is not provided here.
